 {"id":"brenner_bot-59rs","title":"E2E Tests: Full Session Lifecycle with Real Agent Mail","description":"# E2E Tests: Full Session Lifecycle

## Current Gap
Session creation form submission is mocked - no real Agent Mail messages sent.
No tests for the complete flow: create -> agents respond -> compile -> view artifact.

## Goal
E2E tests that exercise the complete session workflow with real (but test) Agent Mail.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Session Creation Flow
```typescript
test("creates session and receives agent responses", async ({ page }) => {
  // Navigate to session creation
  await page.goto("/sessions/new");
  
  // Fill form with test data
  await page.fill('[name="threadId"]', 'E2E-TEST-001');
  await page.fill('[name="excerpt"]', '§42: Test excerpt content...');
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Add Recipient' }).click();
  
  // Submit (real API call to test Agent Mail)
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Start Session' }).click();
  
  // Verify redirect to session page
  await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/sessions\/E2E-TEST-001/);
  
  // Verify kickoff message visible in thread
  await expect(page.getByText('KICKOFF')).toBeVisible();
});
```

### 2. Agent Response Simulation
```typescript
test("displays agent responses in thread view", async ({ page }) => {
  // Pre-seed test data in Agent Mail
  await seedTestSession('E2E-TEST-002', {
    messages: [
      { from: 'Orchestrator', subject: 'KICKOFF', body: '...' },
      { from: 'TestAgent1', subject: 'Re: KICKOFF', body: '```delta\n...\n```' }
    ]
  });
  
  await page.goto("/sessions/E2E-TEST-002");
  
  // Verify thread displays messages
  await expect(page.getByText('TestAgent1')).toBeVisible();
  await expect(page.locator('.delta-block')).toBeVisible();
});
```

### 3. Artifact Compilation
```typescript
test("compiles artifact from agent deltas", async ({ page }) => {
  await seedTestSession('E2E-TEST-003', { /* messages with deltas */ });
  
  await page.goto("/sessions/E2E-TEST-003");
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Compile Artifact' }).click();
  
  // Verify artifact sections appear
  await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Hypothesis Slate' })).toBeVisible();
  await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="hypothesis-item"]')).toHaveCount(3);
});
```

### 4. Evidence Review Flow
```typescript
test("navigates to evidence review page", async ({ page }) => {
  await seedTestSession('E2E-TEST-004', { hasArtifact: true });
  
  await page.goto("/sessions/E2E-TEST-004");
  await page.getByRole('link', { name: 'Review Evidence' }).click();
  
  await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/sessions\/E2E-TEST-004\/evidence/);
  await expect(page.getByText('Evidence Pack')).toBeVisible();
});
```

## Infrastructure Needed

### E2E Test Helpers
```typescript
// e2e/utils/agent-mail-seeder.ts
export async function seedTestSession(threadId: string, config: SessionConfig): Promise<void>;
export async function cleanupTestSession(threadId: string): Promise<void>;
export async function getTestServerUrl(): string;
```

### Playwright Fixture Extension
```typescript
// e2e/utils/test-fixtures.ts
export const test = base.extend<{
  testSession: { seed: typeof seedTestSession; cleanup: typeof cleanupTestSession };
}>({
  testSession: async ({}, use) => {
    const sessions: string[] = [];
    await use({
      seed: async (id, config) => { sessions.push(id); return seedTestSession(id, config); },
      cleanup: async (id) => cleanupTestSession(id)
    });
    // Cleanup all seeded sessions after test
    for (const id of sessions) await cleanupTestSession(id);
  }
});
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log all Agent Mail API calls with request/response
- Log session state transitions
- Capture network waterfall for session creation
- Attach thread data to test report on failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session creation submits to real test Agent Mail
- [ ] Agent response simulation works
- [ ] Artifact compilation tested end-to-end
- [ ] Evidence review page tested
- [ ] All tests have detailed logging
- [ ] Tests cleanup seeded data after run

## Depends On
- brenner_bot-h909 (Agent Mail Test Server)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5aw","title":"Web: /corpus/[doc] should 404 on unknown doc","description":"## Problem
`/corpus/[doc]` assumes the route param is a valid `CORPUS_DOCS` entry. If someone navigates to an unknown doc ID, the server prefetch (`readCorpusDoc`) throws and the UI renders an error state instead of a proper 404.

## Why this matters
First principles:
- Unknown resources should return 404 (not a generic error UI).
- Prevents leaking internal details in error messages and improves UX.

## Root cause
`apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx` prefetches via `readCorpusDoc(docId)` without validating `docId` against `CORPUS_DOCS` and without calling `notFound()`.

## Fix
- In the server page component, validate `docId` against `CORPUS_DOCS`.
- If missing, call `notFound()`.

## Acceptance
- Visiting `/corpus/__does_not_exist__` returns Next.js 404.
- Normal doc routes still hydrate and render.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5i2","title":"Task: Evaluate and select search library","description":"Compare search library options and select the best fit.

## Purpose

Make an informed decision on search library before implementation. Document the decision for future reference.

## Candidates

### FlexSearch
- **Pros**: Fastest, async search, memory efficient, used by VS Code
- **Cons**: Larger bundle (~20KB), more complex API
- **Best for**: Large datasets, performance-critical apps

### MiniSearch
- **Pros**: Small bundle (~7KB), simple API, good relevance
- **Cons**: Slower than FlexSearch on large datasets
- **Best for**: Smaller datasets, simplicity-focused

### Fuse.js
- **Pros**: Fuzzy search built-in, very popular
- **Cons**: Slower, fuzzy may not be what we want
- **Best for**: Typo-tolerant search

### lunr.js
- **Pros**: Stemming, TF-IDF ranking, mature
- **Cons**: Larger index size, slower build
- **Best for**: Traditional full-text search

## Evaluation Criteria

1. **Search speed**: Time to return results for typical query
2. **Index size**: Compressed size for our ~68k word corpus
3. **Bundle size**: Impact on JS bundle
4. **Relevance**: Quality of ranking results
5. **API simplicity**: Ease of integration
6. **Maintenance**: Active development, community

## Evaluation Process

1. Install each library in test environment
2. Build index from corpus
3. Run same queries, measure time and relevance
4. Compare index sizes
5. Document results in comparison table

## Deliverable

Decision document with:
- Comparison table
- Benchmark results
- Recommended library
- Rationale for choice

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] At least 3 libraries evaluated
- [ ] Benchmarks run on actual corpus
- [ ] Decision documented with rationale
- [ ] Selected library installed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:29:19.187446304-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:53:47.505468958-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:53:47.505468958-05:00","close_reason":"Evaluated FlexSearch, MiniSearch, and Fuse.js. Selected MiniSearch for best balance of speed (0.89ms), recall, bundle size (7KB), and search-as-you-type support. Decision documented in specs/search-library-decision.md.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5i2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-gnj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:30:22.346830574-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-5kr7","title":"Feature: Assumption Registry and Lifecycle","description":"# Feature: Assumption Registry and Lifecycle

## Purpose
Track assumptions as first-class entities with lifecycle states, dependency links, and scale/physics checks. This fills a CRITICAL gap in the current design.

## Why This Is Needed

The Brenner method emphasizes:
- §58: "The imprisoned imagination" — scale constraints are load-bearing assumptions
- §147: Exclusion requires knowing WHAT you're assuming
- Artifact Schema Section 5: Assumption Ledger with Statement, Load, Test, Status

Current gap: The Research Intelligence System tracks hypotheses and tests but **completely ignores assumptions**. This means:
- We can't answer "what assumptions does H1 depend on?"
- We can't propagate invalidation (if A1 is wrong, which hypotheses are affected?)
- The Scorecard's "Assumption Tracking" dimension has no data source!

## Schema Design

```typescript
export const AssumptionTypeSchema = z.enum([
  'background',       // Domain knowledge assumed true
  'methodological',   // Assumptions about experimental approach
  'boundary',         // Scope/applicability assumptions
  'scale_physics',    // Physical/mathematical constraints (MANDATORY to have at least one!)
]);

export const AssumptionStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'unchecked',   // Not yet validated
  'challenged',  // Someone has questioned it
  'verified',    // Evidence supports it
  'falsified',   // Evidence contradicts it
]);

export const AssumptionSchema = z.object({
  /** Stable ID: A-{session_id}-{sequence} */
  id: z.string().regex(/^A-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  
  /** What are we assuming is true? */
  statement: z.string().min(10),
  
  /** Type of assumption */
  type: AssumptionTypeSchema,
  
  /** What breaks if this is wrong? (hypotheses, tests, conclusions) */
  load: z.object({
    affectedHypotheses: z.array(z.string()),  // H-xxx IDs
    affectedTests: z.array(z.string()),        // T-xxx IDs
    description: z.string(),                    // Human explanation
  }),
  
  /** How could this assumption be checked? */
  testMethod: z.string().optional(),
  
  /** Current status */
  status: AssumptionStatusSchema,
  
  /** For scale_physics type: the actual calculation */
  calculation: z.object({
    quantities: z.string(),      // "D ≈ 10 μm²/s, L ≈ 100 μm"
    result: z.string(),          // "τ ≈ L²/D ≈ 1000s ≈ 17 min"
    units: z.string(),           // "seconds, micrometers"
    implication: z.string(),     // "Gradient-based signaling is physically plausible"
    whatItRulesOut: z.string().optional(),  // "Rules out H3 if τ > cell cycle"
  }).optional(),
  
  /** §n transcript anchors */
  anchors: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  
  /** Session where this was recorded */
  sessionId: z.string(),
  
  /** Timestamps */
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  
  /** Free-form notes */
  notes: z.string().optional(),
});
```

## Assumption Lifecycle

```
                    ┌─────────────┐
                    │  unchecked  │  (default state)
                    └──────┬──────┘
                           │
            ┌──────────────┼──────────────┐
            │              │              │
            ▼              ▼              ▼
     ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐
     │  challenged │ │  verified   │ │  falsified  │
     └──────┬──────┘ └─────────────┘ └──────┬──────┘
            │                               │
            └───────────────────────────────┘
                           │
                           ▼
                  [Propagate to dependents]
```

When an assumption is **falsified**:
1. All linked hypotheses get flagged as "assumption-undermined"
2. All linked tests get flagged as "assumption-invalidated"
3. User must decide: revise hypothesis, re-evaluate test, or accept risk

## Scale Check Requirement

From the artifact schema and Brenner methodology, **every research program MUST have at least one scale/physics assumption**. This is the "imprisoned imagination" constraint.

The scorecard's "Scale Check Rigor" dimension (0-3) evaluates:
- 0: No scale check or hand-waved
- 1: Qualitative reasoning without calculation
- 2: Calculation present but incomplete
- 3: Full calculation with units, result, and implications

## CLI Commands

- `brenner assumption list [--status=unchecked|challenged|verified|falsified]`
- `brenner assumption show <id>`
- `brenner assumption create --type=scale_physics --statement="..." --calculation="..."`
- `brenner assumption challenge <id> --reason="..."`
- `brenner assumption verify <id> --evidence="..."`
- `brenner assumption falsify <id> --evidence="..."` → propagates to dependents!
- `brenner assumption link <assumption-id> <hypothesis-id>` — Add dependency

## Integration Points

- **Hypothesis Registry**: Hypotheses gain `linkedAssumptions` field
- **Test Binding**: Tests gain `requiredAssumptions` field
- **Scorecard**: "Assumption Tracking" dimension now has data source
- **Scorecard**: "Scale Check Rigor" dimension evaluates scale_physics assumptions

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Assumption schema defined with all fields including calculation
- [ ] Lifecycle states tracked
- [ ] Dependency links to hypotheses and tests
- [ ] Falsification propagates to dependents
- [ ] At least one scale_physics assumption enforced per program
- [ ] CLI commands for full CRUD + lifecycle

## Tasks (to be created)

1. Define Assumption Zod Schema
2. Implement Assumption Storage Layer
3. Implement Assumption Lifecycle Transitions
4. Create Assumption CLI Commands
5. Write Assumption Module Unit Tests
6. Integrate with Hypothesis Registry (add linkedAssumptions)
7. Integrate with Test Binding (add requiredAssumptions)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:40.29017925-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T02:47:46.207356337-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T02:47:46.207356337-05:00","close_reason":"All components implemented and tested: schema (17 tests), lifecycle (76 tests), storage (37 tests) - 130 total tests passing","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5kr7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-lm3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T20:05:55.467361376-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-5kr7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:52.467545224-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
